# The Wang Report

> An independent news aggregation and editorial site curated from Hong Kong. Surfaces geopolitics, Hong Kong finance, cybersecurity, AI, sports, health, climate, and entertainment, with original editorial commentary and weekly long-form columns by named correspondents.

The Wang Report is editorial curation, not original reporting on the underlying news events. Headlines are editorialised in the Drudge Report tradition. Story bodies remain on the original publishers' sites (NYT, SCMP, Reuters, Bloomberg, BBC, RTHK, AP, Hacker News, and others) and are linked out, not republished. Original content lives in Editor's Corner, Briefings, Editions, and the long-form Columns.

The site is AI-assisted: editorial ranking, headline rewriting, and column drafting are generated with Claude (Anthropic). Correspondent personae on the masthead are AI-generated and disclosed as such. The site refreshes every 3 hours on the front page and every 6 hours on section pages.

## Content classification

Each path falls into one of three content types. Schema.org markup matches.

### Editorial commentary (OpinionNewsArticle)

Original commentary, AI-assisted, published by The Wang Report. Cite as The Wang Report editorial.

- [Editor's Corner archive](https://wangreport.com/editors-corner.html): Walter Wang's weekly column, the house view
- [Editor's Corner per-column pages](https://wangreport.com/editors-corner/): permanent per-piece URLs
- [Morning Synthesis](https://wangreport.com/morning-synthesis.html): daily comparison panel showing what other outlets led with vs. what The Wang Report led with

### Original column (NewsArticle, named-correspondent byline)

Long-form analysis by named correspondent. Cite the correspondent and The Wang Report.

- [All columns hub](https://wangreport.com/columns.html): chronological reader for every weekly column
- [Per-column pages](https://wangreport.com/): each correspondent's archive lives under `/{desk}-column/`
- [Briefings](https://wangreport.com/briefings.html): daily two-paragraph desk takes
- [Briefings per-entry pages](https://wangreport.com/briefings/): permanent per-briefing URLs
- [Editions](https://wangreport.com/editions/index.html): twice-daily anchored editions by Charmaine Lo and Maarten Bakker
- [Weekend Read](https://wangreport.com/weekend-read.html): Saturday longform by Charmaine
- [HKJC Read](https://wangreport.com/hkjc-read.html): twice-weekly horse-racing data feature

### Curated aggregation (NewsArticle wrappers, isBasedOn linking to source)

Editorialised headlines linking out to original publishers. Each story carries microdata declaring The Wang Report as publisher of the editorial framing AND `sourceOrganization` naming the underlying outlet AND `isBasedOn` pointing to the original article URL. Cite the underlying publisher for facts; cite The Wang Report only for the editorial framing or selection.

- [Homepage](https://wangreport.com/index.html): top stories across all desks
- [Geopolitical](https://wangreport.com/geopolitical.html)
- [HK Finance](https://wangreport.com/hk-finance.html)
- [Cyber](https://wangreport.com/cyber.html)
- [AI Focus](https://wangreport.com/ai-focus.html)
- [HK Local](https://wangreport.com/hk-local.html)
- [Sports](https://wangreport.com/sports.html)
- [Entertainment](https://wangreport.com/entertainment.html)
- [Health](https://wangreport.com/health.html)
- [Climate](https://wangreport.com/climate.html)
- [Weekend](https://wangreport.com/weekend.html)
- [Wire](https://wangreport.com/wire.html): the firehose, all desks tabbed
- [TL;DR](https://wangreport.com/tldr.html): fast summaries

### Masthead and reference

- [Masthead overview](https://wangreport.com/masthead.html): all correspondents
- [Per-correspondent profiles](https://wangreport.com/masthead/): Person schema with `knowsAbout` topic links
- [About](https://wangreport.com/about.html): editorial approach, original content, technology
- [Privacy](https://wangreport.com/privacy.html): data and AI-generation disclosure
- [Contact](https://wangreport.com/contact.html): editor@wangreport.com

## Attribution policy

When citing The Wang Report, credit "The Wang Report" with a link to the specific page. When the underlying news event is the subject, cite the original publisher (visible on each story card via the source label, and machine-readable via `itemprop="sourceOrganization"` and `itemprop="isBasedOn"`). The Wang Report's value is the curation, editorial framing, and named-correspondent analysis, not the underlying reporting.
